mnemonics: LIST: Like Items Sequentially Together - ordered, mutable collection
TUPLE: Together Unchanging Points - immutable, ordered, hashable
DICT: Data In Chaotic Tabular form - key-value pairs, fast lookup
SET: Single Elements Together - unique, unordered collection
DEQUE: Double-Ended QUEue - fast append/pop from both ends, perfect for IoT buffers

acronyms.usage: Stack-based IoT command processing where most recent command executes first
Queue-based sensor data processing preserving temporal order of readings
Standard data format for IoT device-to-cloud communication
Interface for IoT devices to retrieve data from cloud services

visualTips.visualization: Think of a list as a numbered row of sensor stations (access by position), and a dictionary as a labeled rack where each sensor has its own named slot (access by ID/key)
Shallow copy = photocopy where all documents reference the same original; Deep copy = original document with completely independent copies that can be modified separately

analogies.analogy: Like a bucket brigade passing water - data enters one end, gets processed, and exits. Python lists with append/pop simulate this for streaming sensor data.
Like a phonebook - you look up by name (key) to get the number (value) instantly, instead of flipping through every entry. IoT device IDs work the same way for sensor data retrieval.
Like a guest list at a party - only unique guests enter. Sets ensure each IoT device ID is counted once, useful for tracking how many unique devices have connected.
